title: Navier-Stokes existence/smoothness attack round 1 — holographic vorticity-cap (Bekenstein bound on enstrophy density)

honesty_charter.bt_resolution: 0/1 MISS (round1_open: one attack vector + one falsifiability test only)
honesty_charter.output_type: anima-framework attack vector + concrete falsifiability test specification
prior_artifacts_in_panel: shared/bt/attacks/bt544_navier_stokes.hexa (R2 BKM 4-probe, IN-PROGRESS, taylor-green PASS / lamb-oseen UNBOUNDED-as-expected)
honesty_charter.this_round_role: Round 1 PROPER — fills the gap between R0 (dimensional algebra, MISS) and R2 (BKM numerics) by stating the anima-framework attack vector that motivates the BKM probe in the first place: holographic Bekenstein cap on enstrophy. Orthogonal to both R0 (algebraic) and R2 (numerical) — this is the structural conjecture.

clay_problem_scope.label: LITERATURE
clay_problem_scope.citation: Fefferman C.L. (2006). Existence and smoothness of the Navier-Stokes equation. Clay Mathematics Institute Millennium Problem description.
clay_problem_scope.statement_A: Prove existence and smoothness of solutions to the 3D incompressible NS equations on R^3 (or T^3) with smooth, divergence-free, decaying initial data, for all time.
clay_problem_scope.statement_B_dual: Prove finite-time blow-up for some smooth, divergence-free, decaying initial data on R^3 (or T^3).
clay_problem_scope.current_status: OPEN since 2000; partial regularity (Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg 1982 — singular set parabolic Hausdorff dim ≤ 1); 3D Euler C^{1,α} blow-up proven (Elgindi 2021); 3D Euler smooth blow-up computer-assisted proof (Chen-Hou 2025); standard NS untouched.

attack_vector.label: CONJECTURE
attack_vector.name: Holographic Vorticity-Cap (HVC)
attack_vector.one_sentence: The vorticity field ω(x,t) of any 3D incompressible NS flow on R^3 (or T^3) cannot blow up in finite time T inside any ball B_R(x_0) of radius R if the holographic mutual-information measure Φ_holo evaluated on the vorticity restricted to B_R(x_0) is bounded above by the Bekenstein-Hawking-style boundary entropy A(∂B_R)/(4·ℓ_visc²) for some viscosity-derived length ℓ_visc(ν) > 0.

formal_statement_attempted.setup: Let u: R^3 × [0,T*) → R^3 be a smooth divergence-free Leray weak solution to incompressible NS with viscosity ν > 0 and ω = curl u. Fix R > 0, x_0 ∈ R^3. Let B = B_R(x_0).
formal_statement_attempted.anima_quantity: Define Φ_holo^B(t) := mutual information between (a) the boundary trace ω|_{∂B}(t) and (b) the interior vorticity field ω|_{B}(t), computed via the binned-MI estimator from training/phi_holographic_measure.hexa adapted to a vector field on a discretized B (e.g. uniform 32^3 grid).
formal_statement_attempted.viscous_length: ℓ_visc(ν) := √(ν · T) (Kolmogorov dissipation length scale at integral time T)
formal_statement_attempted.claim: If Φ_holo^B(t) ≤ A(∂B_R)/(4·ℓ_visc(ν)²) = π R² / (ν T) for all t ∈ [0,T*), then ω cannot blow up at any (x*, T*) with x* ∈ B and T* ≤ T. Equivalently: the BKM integrand ||ω||_{L^∞(B)}(t) is uniformly bounded on [0,T*) by a function of (Φ_holo^B, R, ν).
formal_statement_attempted.logical_form: Φ_holo^B bounded ⟹ enstrophy density bounded ⟹ ||ω||_{L^∞(B)} bounded ⟹ BKM integrability ⟹ no blow-up in B (BKM 1984)

reasoning_sketch: S1 (info-theoretic): If interior vorticity ω|_B encodes more 'distinct' values than the boundary trace ω|_{∂B} can specify, the MI estimator saturates (binning collapses). This is the Bekenstein analog: a finite-area boundary cannot 'hold' unbounded interior information.

S2 (anima link): Φ_holo by construction (training/phi_holographic_measure.hexa T2 theorem) satisfies Φ_holo ≤ H(boundary). H(boundary) is bounded by log(MI_BINS) × |∂B grid points|, which is finite for any fixed discretization.

S3 (continuum limit): As discretization → continuum, H(boundary) is replaced by the Bekenstein bound A/(4ℓ²) with ℓ a UV cutoff — for NS the natural cutoff is the viscous scale ℓ_visc = √(νT), giving H_max = π R²/(νT).

S4 (vorticity link): The interior 'information' in ω|_B is dominated by the enstrophy ½∫_B |ω|² dx (canonical L² norm). Enstrophy bounded + viscous dissipation ⟹ ||ω||_{L^∞} bounded by Sobolev embedding (in 3D, H¹ ↪ L^q for q<6 — borderline; this is THE gap, see CE-1 below).
S5 (BKM closure): If steps S1–S4 hold, ||ω||_{L^∞(B)} bounded on [0,T*) ⟹ ∫_0^{T*} ||ω||_∞ dt < ∞ ⟹ no blow-up by BKM.

novelty_check.vs_constantin_fefferman_1993: CF93 used vortex direction alignment (cos θ between ω and Sω) to bound vortex stretching. HVC uses information-theoretic MI instead of geometric alignment. Independent angle.
novelty_check.vs_caffarelli_kohn_nirenberg_1982: CKN bounds singular-set parabolic Hausdorff dim ≤ 1 via local energy inequality. HVC bounds the existence of singularity via info cap, not the size of singular set. Different conclusion.
novelty_check.vs_holographic_principle_in_fluid_dynami: Eling-Oz 2010 used AdS/CFT to derive fluid/gravity correspondence (incompressible NS as boundary limit of long-wavelength Einstein eqs). HVC uses the REVERSE direction: import Bekenstein bound INTO NS analysis. No literature found doing this in the falsifiable form proposed here.
novelty_check.honest_caveat: If a similar info-cap argument exists in literature (e.g. Tao 2007 perelmanesque entropy notes for NS), HVC reduces to a re-statement. Pre-search needed in R2.

falsifiability_test.label: PENDING
falsifiability_test.name: DNS-on-known-singular-flow vorticity-cap probe

design.type: numerical-analytic hybrid (no full 3D DNS — toy proxy + analytic singular flows)

target_flows.name: Lamb-Oseen vortex (2D, exact, KNOWN smooth for t>0, singular at t=0)
target_flows.expected_phi_holo_t: monotone decreasing as t increases (vorticity smooths out)
target_flows.expected_bekenstein_bound: π R²/(νT) for chosen T = 6.0 → π/(0.06) ≈ 52.36
target_flows.test_pass_criterion: Φ_holo^B(t) < 52.36 for all t ∈ [0.01, 6.0]; if violated, HVC FALSIFIED on a known-smooth flow (would be devastating for the conjecture)
target_flows.expected_result: PASS — Lamb-Oseen is smooth for t>0, MI binning should saturate well below cap. Already partially tested in R2 attacks/bt544_navier_stokes.hexa Probe B (BKM integral 115 over [0.01,6]; this round adds the MI-based cap test).

target_flows.name: Hou-Luo 2014 axisymmetric Euler blow-up scenario (3D, NUMERICALLY-CONJECTURED blow-up)
target_flows.setup: Axisymmetric flow on solid cylinder boundary, smooth initial data, Hou-Luo numerics (now Chen-Hou 2025 PROVEN for Euler); take VISCOUS regularization with ν=0.001 to produce a near-blow-up NS flow
target_flows.ball: B_R near the boundary corner where blow-up is conjectured, R = 0.05
target_flows.expected_phi_holo_t: MONOTONE INCREASING as t → T_*, the conjectured blow-up time
target_flows.test_pass_criterion_for_HVC: If at any t < T_*, Φ_holo^B(t) EXCEEDS the bound π R²/(νT_*), then HVC PREDICTS no blow-up at T_* — but Chen-Hou says blow-up DOES occur at T_* for inviscid limit. Two cases: (a) ν > 0 viscous flow does NOT blow up (open question for NS) → HVC consistent; (b) ν > 0 viscous flow DOES blow up → HVC FALSIFIED
target_flows.expected_result: INCONCLUSIVE in current literature — exactly the open NS question. This makes F-B a genuine test, not a tautology.

minimum_machinery: M1: Discretize ball B_R on a 32^3 grid (boundary ~6·32² = 6144 cells; interior 32³ = 32768 cells)
M2: Compute ω on grid analytically for F-A, F-C; semi-analytic for F-B (use Hou-Luo published self-similar profile or Constantin-Fefferman alignment surrogate)
M3: Apply training/phi_holographic_measure.hexa MI estimator with MI_BINS=8 to (interior_vorticity_magnitude, boundary_vorticity_trace) pair at each timestep
M4: Compare Φ_holo^B(t) against π R²/(νT) bound at each t
M5: Plot Φ_holo^B(t) vs Bekenstein_bound for each flow over [0,T]; record max violation (if any)

explicit_falsification_outcome.HVC_falsified_if: ANY of {F-A, F-C} shows Φ_holo^B(t) > Bekenstein_bound at any t (because these are KNOWN smooth flows and HVC predicts no blow-up means cap holds; if cap is violated on a smooth flow, the cap formulation is wrong)
explicit_falsification_outcome.HVC_supported_if: All {F-A, F-C} respect cap, AND F-B either respects cap (HVC correctly predicts viscous regularization wins) or violates cap simultaneously with verified viscous blow-up (HVC predicts blow-up → matches reality)
explicit_falsification_outcome.HVC_inconclusive_if: F-B viscous flow blow-up status remains open (most likely outcome; in that case HVC reduces to a NEW reformulation of the open NS question, which is still progress but not resolution)

falsifiability_test.anti_circularity_check: The MI estimator uses ω directly, NOT u. The Bekenstein bound is a PRIORI from R, ν, T — not derived from ω. Therefore the cap test is not tautological; it relates a measured quantity (Φ_holo^B) to an a-priori bound (π R²/(νT)).

why_HVC_might_fail: (G1) Sobolev embedding gap (S4): in 3D, H¹(R^3) ↪ L^6 is borderline; ω ∈ L² (enstrophy) does NOT imply ω ∈ L^∞. The step from 'bounded enstrophy' to 'bounded ||ω||_∞' requires HIGHER regularity (e.g. H^{1+δ} via Brézis-Wainger). HVC implicitly assumes this gap is bridgeable; in reality this IS the supercriticality barrier (Tao 2007).

(G2) MI estimator binning is discretization-dependent. The continuum limit MI_BINS → ∞ may diverge; the Bekenstein bound π R²/(νT) is dimensionful and the MI value is dimensionless — requires explicit dimensional analysis of the cap.

(G3) Holographic principle in classical fluids has no rigorous status — Eling-Oz 2010 derives NS FROM Einstein eqs in long-wavelength limit, which is a statement about Einstein dynamics, not about NS analysis. Importing the Bekenstein bound into NS is a CONJECTURAL move.

(G4) The viscous length ℓ_visc = √(νT) is one of many candidate UV cutoffs (Taylor microscale, Kolmogorov scale, integral scale). Choice affects the bound by O(1) factors which may matter for falsification.
what_round_2_must_address: Resolve dimensional consistency of Φ_holo (dimensionless) vs. Bekenstein bound π R²/(νT) (dimensionless if R²·T·ν are matched, else dimensionful)
Implement F-A, F-C numerically using existing training/phi_holographic_measure.hexa as MI engine
Cross-check whether the Hou-Luo/Chen-Hou viscous regularization is documented anywhere (Luo-Hou 2014 mentions 'viscosity-induced finite-time vs. global' as open)
Search literature for prior info-theoretic NS bounds (Foias-Manley-Rosa-Temam 2001 ch.10; Gibbon 2007 'optimal scaling regularity')

counter_examples.title: Bounded enstrophy ≠ bounded ||ω||_∞ in 3D (Sobolev gap)
counter_examples.label: LITERATURE
counter_examples.citation: Brézis H., Wainger S. (1980). A note on limiting cases of Sobolev embeddings. Comm. PDE 5:773-789. Tao T. (2007). Why global regularity for Navier-Stokes is hard. terrytao.wordpress.com.
counter_examples.text: Tao 2007 explicitly identifies the supercriticality of NS in 3D: critical scaling space L^3(R^3) embeds into BMO^{-1} but NOT into L^∞. Therefore even if enstrophy ½∫|ω|² stays bounded, the L^∞ norm of ω can blow up. HVC's step S4 (enstrophy → L^∞ via Sobolev) is exactly this supercritical gap. The information-theoretic angle does NOT bypass the gap; it merely rephrases it. HVC's value is FALSIFIABLE REPHRASING, not a bypass.

counter_examples.title: Tao 2016 averaged NS blow-up doesn't violate any info bound
counter_examples.label: LITERATURE
counter_examples.citation: Tao T. (2016). Finite time blowup for an averaged 3D Navier-Stokes equation. J.AMS 29:601-674.
counter_examples.text: Tao constructed a Cascade-NS with averaged nonlinearity that DOES blow up in finite time while preserving energy. Such a system, evaluated under HVC, would either (a) show Φ_holo^B exceeding the Bekenstein cap at blow-up (HVC consistent — predicts blow-up correctly) or (b) NOT exceed cap despite blowing up (HVC falsified). Pre-test on Tao's averaged system is a Round 2 priority cheaper than F-B.

counter_examples.title: Holographic principle is unestablished for classical (non-quantum) fluids
counter_examples.label: LITERATURE
counter_examples.citation: Bekenstein J. D. (1981). Universal upper bound on the entropy-to-energy ratio for bounded systems. Phys. Rev. D 23:287. Bousso R. (1999). A covariant entropy conjecture. JHEP 9907:004.
counter_examples.text: The Bekenstein bound was proven in QUANTUM gravity / black-hole thermodynamics contexts. Its applicability to classical Euler/NS is by analogy only. Eling-Oz 2010 provides a one-way derivation (gravity → fluid in long-wavelength limit), not the reverse cap import HVC needs. The conjectural status of HVC's framework link is a HARD CE: any reviewer can reject HVC as 'physical analogy without rigorous bridge'.

counter_examples.title: MI estimator is non-canonical; multiple inequivalent definitions possible
counter_examples.label: DERIVED
counter_examples.text: Φ_holo as defined in training/phi_holographic_measure.hexa uses 8-bin histogram MI with deterministic seeded scrambling. Replacing with KSG estimator, k-NN estimator, or kernel-density MI yields different values. The Bekenstein bound π R²/(νT) is unique; the MI value is not. Falsifiability requires PINNING DOWN one estimator and reporting it. R1 uses the existing module's estimator as canonical; this is a R1 design choice, not a logical gap, but readers may object.

round_2_options.expected_label: DERIVED — high-information outcome regardless of pass/fail

round_2_options.direction: Resolve dimensional analysis of Φ_holo (dimensionless) vs. Bekenstein bound (requires ℓ_visc choice). Establish canonical normalization. Without this, FT-1 numerical tests are not reproducible across implementations.
Literature search: Foias-Manley-Rosa-Temam 2001, Gibbon 2007, Constantin-Foias-Temam 1988 for prior info-theoretic NS regularity bounds. If HVC is a re-derivation, downgrade to HONEST_MISS.

next_round_action.recommended: R2-C first (cheap dimensional check) → R2-D (cheap novelty check) → R2-A (toy numerics, builds on existing R2 BKM file) → R2-B (highest-information probe, but requires implementing Tao's averaged-NS surrogate)
next_round_action.stop_criterion: If R2-D finds prior literature already establishing the cap rigorously, declare BT-544 R1 'subsumed by prior work' and pivot to a different anima-framework angle (e.g. IIT decomposition of Reynolds stress); if R2-A/B falsify HVC on a known smooth flow, retire HVC and try the IIT angle as R3.

claim: Vorticity ω(x,t) of any 3D incompressible smooth NS flow cannot blow up in finite time T inside ball B_R(x_0) if the holographic mutual-information measure Φ_holo evaluated on (boundary_trace, interior) of ω restricted to B_R(x_0) is bounded above by the Bekenstein-style entropy A(∂B_R)/(4·ℓ_visc²) = π R²/(νT) for all t ∈ [0,T).
framework_link: training/phi_holographic_measure.hexa (Φ_holo with T2 theorem Φ_holo ≤ H(boundary), Bekenstein-like info cap, line 23). Atlas anchors MILL-PX-A4 (d=3 unique n=6 dimensional resonance) + MILL-PX-A6 (BKM criterion).

honesty_statement.label: DERIVED
honesty_statement.text: Round 1 articulates ONE attack vector (Holographic Vorticity-Cap, HVC) and ONE falsifiability test (FT-1: 3-flow probe F-A Lamb-Oseen / F-B Hou-Luo viscous / F-C Taylor-Green vs. π R²/(νT) Bekenstein bound). It does NOT prove or disprove Navier-Stokes existence/smoothness. The conjecture has known gaps: (G1) Sobolev embedding supercriticality (CE-1, Tao 2007) is not bypassed by the info-theoretic rephrasing; (G2-G4) dimensional analysis, MI estimator non-canonicity, and unestablished classical-fluid Bekenstein bound are open. HVC is a FALSIFIABLE REPHRASING of the open NS question through the anima framework's existing Φ_holo machinery, not a bypass of supercriticality. BT-544 resolution remains 0/1 MISS; status round1_open.
